overview.tagline: The Retail Capital of Bergen County
overview.description: Paramus is a borough in Bergen County, New Jersey, renowned as one of the top retail destinations in the United States. Home to several major shopping centers including Westfield Garden State Plaza and Paramus Park, the borough generates more retail sales per square mile than almost any other zip code in America. Despite its commercial prominence, Paramus maintains a strong residential character with tree-lined streets and well-regarded schools. The borough is famously known for its blue laws, which restrict most retail activity on Sundays.
highlights: Home to Westfield Garden State Plaza, one of the largest malls in the US
Famous blue laws restrict most retail on Sundays
Over $6 billion in annual retail sales
Van Saun County Park with Bergen County Zoo

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Borough Tax Assessor. Reassessments occur periodically; last full revaluation was completed in recent years.
realEstate.recentTrends: Paramus home prices have seen steady appreciation, driven by strong schools, retail convenience, and proximity to NYC. Demand remains high for single-family homes, particularly in the eastern sections near Ridgewood.
neighborhoodNotes: Eastern Paramus near Ridgewood border is among the most desirable residential areas
The Spring Valley section features larger lots and mature trees
Areas near Route 4 and Route 17 are more commercial in character
The Westfield Garden State Plaza area has seen mixed-use development proposals
South Paramus near Van Saun Park is popular with families

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash collection is provided twice weekly by a contracted hauler for all residential properties.
details: Collection days vary by neighborhood; check the borough website for your schedule
Trash must be placed at the curb by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use sealed containers or approved trash bags
Bulk pickup is available on scheduled dates throughout the year
Holiday schedule adjustments are posted on the borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Single-stream curbside recycling is collected weekly. The borough participates in the Bergen County Utilities Authority recycling program.
details: Recycling is collected once per week
Accepted materials: paper, cardboard, glass bottles/jars, plastic #1-#7, aluminum and steel cans
No plastic bags, styrofoam, or food-contaminated materials
Electronics recycling events are held periodically
Yard waste is collected separately during spring and fall

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is supplied by Veolia Water (formerly United Water/SUEZ). Sewer service is managed by the borough with treatment by the Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Water bills are issued quarterly by Veolia Water
Sewer charges appear on the borough tax bill
Water quality reports are published annually
Contact Veolia for new connections, leaks, or billing issues
Fire hydrant flushing occurs in spring and fall

permits.title: Building & Zoning Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles all construction permits, zoning applications, and code enforcement for residential and commercial properties.
details: Permits required for most construction, renovation, and signage
Applications available online or at Borough Hall
Zoning Board of Adjustment meets monthly for variance requests
Planning Board reviews site plans and subdivision applications
Certificate of Occupancy required before occupying new or renovated structures

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Paramus does not have metered street parking in residential areas. Commercial parking is managed by individual shopping centers and businesses.
details: No municipal parking meters
Overnight parking restrictions apply on borough streets
Shopping center parking is free but privately managed
No resident parking permit program
Street parking regulations enforced by Paramus Police

additionalServices.title: Leaf Collection
additionalServices.description: Seasonal leaf collection is provided in the fall for all residential properties.
details: Loose leaf collection runs from mid-October through mid-December
Rake leaves to the curb; do not place in bags during loose leaf season
Do not mix leaves with branches or other debris
Collection schedule is posted on the borough website

additionalServices.title: Snow Removal
additionalServices.description: The Department of Public Works handles snow plowing for borough streets.
details: Borough streets are plowed during and after significant snowfall
Residents must clear sidewalks within 24 hours after snow stops
Do not push snow into the street
Fire hydrants must be kept clear of snow

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Paramus enforces noise regulations to maintain residential quality of life, with specific quiet hours and restrictions on construction noise.
keyRules: Quiet hours are from 10:00 PM to 7:00 AM on weekdays and 10:00 PM to 9:00 AM on weekends
Construction activity is prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 6:00 PM on weekdays
No construction on Sundays or federal holidays
Leaf blowers and power equipment restricted during quiet hours
Excessive noise complaints are handled by Paramus Police

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties in good condition, including lawns, structures, and exterior appearances.
keyRules: Grass and weeds must not exceed 10 inches in height
Properties must be free of accumulated rubbish and debris
Fences must be maintained in good repair
Swimming pools must be properly fenced and maintained
Vacant properties must be secured and maintained
propertyMaintenance.source: Borough of Paramus Code, Property Maintenance Chapter

pets.title: Pet & Animal Regulations
pets.summary: All dogs and cats must be licensed and vaccinated. Paramus has leash laws and regulations on the number of pets per household.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually
Cats must be licensed and vaccinated against rabies
Dogs must be on a leash at all times when off the owner's property
Pet owners must clean up after their animals on public and private property
Maximum of 3 dogs or 3 cats per household without a kennel license
Barking dog complaints are investigated by Animal Control

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Street parking is regulated throughout the borough with restrictions on overnight parking and commercial vehicles.
keyRules: No overnight street parking between 2:00 AM and 6:00 AM without a permit
Commercial vehicles over 4 tons are prohibited from parking on residential streets
No parking within 25 feet of a stop sign or crosswalk
Vehicles may not be parked on front lawns
Abandoned vehicles will be towed at the owner's expense
parking.source: Borough of Paramus Code, Traffic Chapter

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must comply with borough housing codes and landlords must register rental units.
keyRules: Rental properties must meet all housing code requirements
Landlords must register rental units with the borough
Certificate of Habitability required before occupancy
Inspections may be required at change of tenancy
Multi-family zoning restrictions apply in most residential zones
rentals.source: Borough of Paramus Code, Housing Chapter

other.title: Blue Laws (Sunday Closing)
other.summary: Paramus is one of the few remaining municipalities in the US with strict blue laws prohibiting most retail sales on Sundays.
keyRules: Most retail stores must remain closed on Sundays
Exceptions include restaurants, gas stations, pharmacies, and convenience stores
Car dealerships cannot sell vehicles on Sundays
Grocery stores and food establishments may operate on Sundays
Violations are subject to fines and enforcement action
The blue laws have been upheld repeatedly and remain strongly supported by residents
other.source: Bergen County Blue Laws; Paramus Municipal Ordinance

other.title: Fence Regulations
other.summary: Fences are regulated by height, material, and setback requirements depending on their location on the property.
keyRules: Front yard fences may not exceed 4 feet in height
Rear and side yard fences may not exceed 6 feet in height
A zoning permit is required before installing any fence
Chain-link fences are permitted but must be maintained
Fence must be installed with the finished side facing outward

publicSchools.name: Midland School
publicSchools.notes: Located in the southern section of Paramus

publicSchools.name: Ridge Ranch School
publicSchools.notes: Located in the eastern section near Ridgewood border

publicSchools.name: West Brook Middle School
publicSchools.notes: One of two middle schools in the district

publicSchools.name: East Brook Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Serves the eastern half of the borough

publicSchools.name: Paramus High School
publicSchools.notes: Comprehensive high school with strong academics and athletics; home of the Spartans

privateSchools.name: Paramus Catholic High School
privateSchools.notes: Roman Catholic coeducational school; strong athletics program and college prep curriculum

privateSchools.name: Bergen County Academies
privateSchools.notes: Magnet school located in Hackensack; many Paramus students attend via competitive admission

nearbyHigherEd: Bergen Community College (Paramus) — 2-year public college located in the borough
Ramapo College of New Jersey (Mahwah) — 20 miles

whyMoveHere: Unparalleled shopping convenience with major retail centers minutes away
Strong public school system with high graduation rates
Sunday blue laws create a peaceful residential atmosphere one day per week
Excellent highway access via Routes 4, 17, and the Garden State Parkway
Beautiful Van Saun County Park with zoo and recreation
Active community with numerous youth sports and recreation programs

commuteToNYC.notes: Multiple NJ Transit bus routes serve Paramus with direct service to the Port Authority Bus Terminal in Midtown Manhattan. Route 163 is the most popular. Park-and-ride facilities are available. Driving to the George Washington Bridge takes approximately 20-30 minutes without traffic.

costOfLiving.summary: Paramus has a high cost of living driven primarily by property taxes and housing costs, though it is relatively moderate compared to some neighboring Bergen County towns.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: About 15% above the New Jersey average

pros: World-class shopping and retail within minutes
Strong public schools with good test scores
Quiet Sundays thanks to blue laws
Excellent park system including Van Saun Park
Relatively moderate home prices for Bergen County
cons: Heavy traffic on Routes 4 and 17, especially on Saturdays
High property taxes typical of Bergen County
Commercial development along highways can feel congested
Limited nightlife and walkable downtown area
Blue laws restrict Sunday shopping convenience

restaurants.name: The Capital Grille
restaurants.description: Upscale steakhouse in the Garden State Plaza offering dry-aged steaks, fresh seafood, and an extensive wine list in an elegant setting.

restaurants.name: Seasons 52
restaurants.description: Fresh grill and wine bar featuring a seasonally inspired menu with dishes under 595 calories, located at the Garden State Plaza.

restaurants.name: Chakra
restaurants.description: Modern Indian restaurant offering inventive takes on traditional dishes in a stylish, contemporary atmosphere.

restaurants.name: Venetian
restaurants.description: Classic Italian-American restaurant and catering hall that has been a Paramus staple for decades, known for generous portions.

restaurants.name: Segovia Steakhouse
restaurants.description: Spanish and Portuguese steakhouse famous for its rodizio-style service and Iberian specialties.

parks.name: Van Saun County Park
parks.description: A 146-acre Bergen County park featuring the Bergen County Zoo, train rides, a carousel, playgrounds, picnic areas, and athletic fields. One of the most popular parks in the county.

parks.name: Saddle River County Park (Dunkerhook Area)
parks.description: Part of the extensive Saddle River County Park system running along the Saddle River, offering walking trails, fishing, and nature observation.
parks.address: GS Parkway & Midland Ave, Paramus, NJ 07652

parks.name: Memorial Park
parks.description: Municipal park with athletic fields, tennis courts, a community pool, and playgrounds. Home to many of the borough's youth sports leagues.

parks.name: Cliff Gennarelli-Sportsplex at Paramus
parks.description: Indoor sports complex offering ice skating, fitness center, basketball, and various recreation programs for residents.

events.name: Paramus Independence Day Celebration
events.description: Annual July 4th celebration featuring live music, food vendors, carnival rides, and a spectacular fireworks display at Cliff Gennarelli Sportsplex.

events.name: Paramus Carnival
events.description: Annual spring carnival with rides, games, and food, held as a community fundraiser.

events.name: Bergen County Zoo Events
events.description: Year-round events at the Bergen County Zoo in Van Saun Park, including Zoo Boo (October), Zoo at Night, and seasonal educational programs.

events.name: Paramus 5K Run
events.description: Annual community 5K road race drawing hundreds of participants through the borough's residential streets.

attractions.name: Westfield Garden State Plaza
attractions.description: One of the largest and most profitable shopping malls in the United States with over 300 stores including Nordstrom, Neiman Marcus, and Macy's.

attractions.name: Paramus Park Mall
attractions.description: Regional shopping center with a mix of national retailers and dining options in a convenient location along Route 17.

attractions.name: Bergen County Zoo
attractions.description: Free-admission zoo within Van Saun County Park featuring over 200 animals from around the world, including a reptile house and a native species exhibit.

government.meetingSchedule: Regular meetings are held on the 2nd and 4th Tuesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Paramus Borough Hall, 1 Jockish Square, Paramus, NJ 07652

fire.address: Paramus Fire Department, East 44 Midland Ave, Paramus, NJ 07652

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital (The Valley Hospital)
hospitals.address: 223 N Van Dien Ave, Ridgewood, NJ 07450
